recommendations: python3_file_write_via_bash_bypass requires structural intervention: two instances per session across consecutive sessions indicates the bulk-file pattern has no Write-tool equivalent that feels viable — consider either (a) accepting it as a deliberate tool-use exemption and formalizing the carve-out, or (b) building a multi-file Write wrapper that preserves audit trail; current ad-hoc tolerance creates ambiguity about when the bypass is appropriate

shell_tool_preference_violations has shown zero improvement trajectory across five consecutive calibration cycles at 55-65% rate — this is no longer a habit gap but a structural default; the bias is unlikely to respond to repeated flagging; recommend evaluating whether the underlying tool preference rule needs to be relaxed for compound discovery workflows (ls+find+wc combinations) where no dedicated tool equivalent exists, rather than continuing to flag a rule that is not being followed

optimistic_edit_without_read sub-instance count hit a recorded high (11 sub-instances across 6 units) in this batch — this is the one metric showing genuine worsening rather than stable persistence; the root cause is consistent: Edit attempted on files not read in the current unit; a pre-Edit Read is a 2-second operation that eliminates 100% of these failures; this is the highest-leverage single behavioral change available in the current data
